page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180 is a general-purpose server instance featuring 180 vCPUs and 720 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance provides 90 physical cores with multithreading enabled at two threads per core and dedicated CPU allocation. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, alongside cryptographic acceleration features like AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark evaluations place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory performance is also highly rated, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 13454.22 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth at 356.6 GB/sec. The instance excels in specialized compute tasks, scoring in the top tier for software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and cryptographic operations. It also demonstrates top-tier performance in Redis SET operations, handling 211.43 million operations per second, and shows strong capabilities in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models.

From a resource density perspective, the c3d-standard-180 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for massive parallel workloads that require substantial memory bandwidth and dedicated compute resources without the overhead of GPU hardware. The balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly suitable for large-scale general-purpose and compute-heavy applications. Recommended workloads include software compilation, ray tracing, high-throughput Redis caching, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180 is a general-purpose server configured with 180 vCPUs and 720 GB of RAM, utilizing dedicated AMD EPYC 9B14 processors on the x86_64 architecture. Featuring 90 physical cores with multithreading, it supports advanced instructions including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. The instance does not bundle local storage or GPUs. In benchmark testing, it delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, software compilation, and Redis operations, processing 211.43 million SET operations per second. It also shows strong performance in LLM inference and database operations. Given its high compute density and balanced memory ratio, the c3d-standard-180 is highly efficient for parallelized workloads. It is best suited for software compilation, database management, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180 is a general-purpose server with 180 vCPUs, 720 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC 9B14 processors, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180 features 180 vCPUs and 720 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core x86 platform delivering exceptional memory bandwidth and dedicated compute power for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors with 180 dedicated vCPUs and 720 GB RAM
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth exceeding 13,000 GB/sec cached
Supports AVX-512, cryptographic acceleration, and advanced vector instructions
Excellent throughput for Redis operations and CPU-based LLM inference
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving
